jobs: General
A weathered strategist commanding from the hilltop, making the calls that decide battles.
Marshal
A campaign-hardened leader coordinating entire theaters of war from the field.
Field Marshal
A war-council commander directing all military forces of the realm from the head seat.
Admiral
A sea-salt-stiffened commander plotting naval strategy from the flagship's deck.
Colonel
A regiment-drilling officer of hundreds, honing them for march and battle.
Captain
A sword-belted leader running a company of soldiers, handling discipline, pay, and formation.
Lieutenant
A duty-bound second carrying out the captain's orders and stepping up when he falls.
Sergeant
A bark-voiced enforcer keeping a squad of soldiers in line during the chaos of battle.
Corporal
A fire-team leader relaying sergeants' commands to the front-line ranks.
Commander
A garrison-holding authority with operational control over an outpost or special detachment.
Commodore
A squadron-leading naval officer ranking between captain and rear admiral at sea.
Major
A logistics-minded tactician managing a battalion's needs under the colonel's command.
Centurion
A vine-staff-gripping officer drilling and leading a century of hardened soldiers.
Legionnaire
A shield-and-javelin professional marching in disciplined formation as part of the legion.
Guardsman
A polished-armor sentinel standing watch as part of an elite protective unit.
Archer
A string-calloused bowman putting arrows into targets at two hundred paces.
Crossbowman
A steel-limbed bolt-loosener cranking and firing from behind fortified walls.
Spearman
A shield-wall holder bracing an iron-tipped spear against the charging line.
Pikeman
An eighteen-foot-pike bracer standing in tight formation against cavalry charges.
Cavalry
A lance-couching rider shattering enemy lines on horseback with thundering impact.
Horseman
A saddle-worn scout patrolling, skirmishing, and delivering messages from the field.
Scout
A light-footed shadow slipping ahead of the army to map terrain and report enemy positions.
Sentry
A night-eyed watcher standing guard at gates and walls, alert for every threat.
Watchman
A lantern-carrying patroller of streets and ramparts, calling the hours and raising alarms.
Town Guard
A spear-resting walker of town gates and market squares, keeping peace among locals.
City Watch
A curfew-enforcing patroller breaking up brawls and hunting criminals through city alleys.
Border Guard
A frontier-patrolling sentry watching mountain passes for smugglers and invaders.
Royal Guard
A blade-drawn defender shielding the monarch's person with unwavering loyalty.
Bodyguard
A shadow-close protector ready day and night to take a blade for their single charge.
Mercenary
A coin-loyal sellsword offering blade-skill to the highest bidder, no questions asked.
Sellsword
A contract-wandering fighter taking strangers' wars for silver and moving on.
Warrior
A scar-mapped fighter trained to wield blade and shield in any terrain or weather.
Soldier
A trench-digging marcher drilling in formation and going where the officers point.
Veteran
A campaign-scarred survivor teaching raw recruits how to stay alive in battle.
Recruit
A nervous newcomer learning to march, swing a sword, and follow orders for the first time.
Bannerman
A colors-carrying rallying point marching the unit's flag into the heart of battle.
Standard Bearer
A battle-flag-hoisting target and beacon of morale, holding the colors high overhead.
Siege Engineer
A trebuchet-building designer of rams and siege towers meant to crack fortress walls.
Sapper
A tunnel-digging demolitionist burrowing beneath enemy walls with pick and black powder.
Quartermaster
A barrel-counting logistician keeping the army fed, armed, and supplied on the march.
Bowyer
A stave-shaping craftsman bending yew and elm into longbows and recurves for archers.
Caravan Guard
A tree-line-watching rider alongside merchant wagons, sword loose in its scabbard.
Sailor
A rigging-climbing, deck-scrubbing seaman hauling lines aboard a vessel under open sky.
Ship Captain
A helm-gripping commander steering a vessel and plotting courses across open water.
Navigator
A star-charting plotter keeping ships on course by compass, sextant, and coastal landmarks.
Paladin
A light-wreathed knight wielding blessed steel against the forces of darkness.
Ranger
A wilderness-hardened tracker living off the land for weeks while hunting enemies.
Corsair
A marque-bearing raider striking enemy shipping under the crown's written authority.
Adventurer
A ruin-delving, monster-fighting fortune seeker chasing treasure for a living.
Explorer
A frontier-crossing mapper of uncharted coastlines, jungles, and mountain ranges.
Tracker
A ground-reading hunter of broken twigs and footprints, following trails others cannot see.
Tournament Marshal
A rule-enforcing organizer of jousts and melees, judging victors at the tourney field.
Flag Bearer
A hilltop-standing signaler waving flags to relay battlefield orders across the lines.
Net Thrower
A weighted-net hurler entangling beasts or enemies for capture rather than the kill.
